author: David Reed
summary: Cycle's largest iteration by change count (4 chgs). chg-1 wraps tracing.py kwargs in extra={...} clearing iter-3 type-ignores. chg-2 adds 3 pediatric cases. chg-3 replaces the iter-3 keyword heuristic in pediatric_complex with an integer 1-5 complexity-score model (honest framing: heuristic-in-score-model-clothing given 4 data points). chg-4 adds the third H2 memory entry (asthma dupilumab) with documented non-override of BOTH the cost check AND the new pediatric_complex check.

changes.description: Switch src/pacca/config/tracing.py to extra={...} wrap. Three logger.info / logger.warning calls used structlog-style kwargs against stdlib logging.Logger; iter-3 chg-1 added # type: ignore[call-arg] markers with a TODO to switch to structlog or wrap with extra={...}. iter-5 chose the wrap path (no new dependency; documented stdlib mechanism). All three call sites updated; three type-ignore markers removed.
changes.failure_pattern: Tech-debt accumulation pattern: type-ignore markers added under a TODO comment are easy to leave indefinitely. iter-3 chg-1's TODO was the trigger event; iter-5 chg-1 is the cleanup.
changes.root_cause: Original tracing.py was written assuming structlog or never tested against stdlib Logger's signature. py.typed (iter-3 chg-1) made mypy walk the import graph and surface the type errors.

evidence.note: All three logger calls now use extra={...}: extra={'endpoint': ...}, extra={'detail': ...}, extra={'service_name': ...}. Type-ignores removed.

changes.why_this_component: Observability scaffolding only; no agent surface modified. Same level as iter-2 chg-5 (model SSOT).
changes.rollback_plan: git revert <sha>. Restoring the structlog-style kwargs returns to the silent-drop runtime behavior + the type-ignore-suppressed mypy errors.

changes.description: Add 3 pediatric cases (GC-023 mild well-controlled, GC-024 moderate Crohn's + failures, GC-025 severe AD different condition) as PEDIATRIC_CASES in tests/clinical/pediatric_cases.py. Together with GC-012, gives the chg-3 complexity-score model 4 pediatric data points across the discriminator's input space. Wired into the live clinical gate loop.
changes.failure_pattern: Pre-iter-5, the entire 22-case dataset had exactly ONE pediatric case (GC-012). One data point cannot found a discriminator; any score model fit would just be the keyword heuristic with extra steps. Closes the data-coverage gap that iter-4's chg-3 deferral note identified.
changes.root_cause: Iter-1 ships a single pediatric case for branch coverage. iter-2 chg-3 added near-miss memory traps but no pediatric expansion. iter-3 chg-1 introduced the pediatric_complex check using a keyword heuristic — defensible with 1 case, insufficient for a score-based discriminator.

evidence.note: 3 new GoldenCase instances in a separate PEDIATRIC_CASES list mirroring the iter-2 NEAR_MISS_CASES file pattern. GOLDEN_CASES stays at 20.
Added pytest>=8.0.0 + pytest-asyncio>=0.23.0 to mypy hook additional_dependencies after the modified test_clinical_accuracy.py surfaced untyped @pytest.mark.asyncio decorator errors in the hook env.

changes.why_this_component: Data-only addition + test wiring; no agent surface. Same level as iter-2 chg-3 near-miss cases.
changes.rollback_plan: git revert <sha>. Removing pediatric_cases.py + reverting the gate loop wiring restores the pre-iter-5 22-case live gate.

changes.description: Replace iter-3 keyword heuristic in _check_pediatric_complex with integer 1-5 complexity-score model. Matches the Settings schema. Weighted sum (age extremes +2, severity tier +0 to +3, 2+ failures +1, comorbidities +1; clamped 1-5). Pediatric threshold = 3. Honest framing: heuristic in score-model clothing given 4 data points — defensibility is per-feature rationale + integer schema, not data fit. All 4 pediatric cases route correctly live.
changes.failure_pattern: iter-3 chg-1's keyword heuristic was defensible at 1 pediatric data point but couldn't justify itself once the dataset expanded. The chg-2 expansion forced the model decision.
changes.root_cause: Real-world pediatric escalation decisions weight multiple factors (age, severity, prior failures, comorbidities). The chg-1 keyword heuristic collapsed all of them into 'severity language matches'. With 4 data points the limits show: GC-024 needs to escalate despite 'moderate' severity language, GC-023 must NOT escalate despite being pediatric.

evidence.note: 23 tests: each weight independently, boundary/clamp behavior, structured vs parser fallback paths, real data points (GC-012 score 4, GC-023 score 2, GC-024 score 4, GC-025 score 5).

GC-012 IN_REVIEW score 4 (preserved); GC-023 AUTO_APPROVED score 5 (judge: 'correctly avoids inappropriate escalation despite pediatric age'); GC-024 IN_REVIEW score 4; GC-025 IN_REVIEW score 5. All 4 pediatric data points correctly routed.

Surfaced by deeper import-chain mypy traversal: __all__ added to authorization.py; base.py _call_with_retry got -> Any return type and Mapping[str, object] tool_def; tenacity @retry got # type: ignore[misc,unused-ignore]; Anthropic create() call got # type: ignore[call-overload,unused-ignore]; bool() cast on APIStatusError check.

changes.why_this_component: Same constraint level as iter-3 chg-1 (pediatric_complex check). The change is an improvement to the policy-enforcement logic, not a new branch.
changes.rollback_plan: git revert <sha>. Reverting restores the iter-3 keyword heuristic. ClinicalCase.complexity_score field stays optional (no migration needed). The chg-2 pediatric cases continue to validate against whatever heuristic is current.

changes.description: Third H2 memory entry: dupilumab for severe eosinophilic asthma. Follows the iter-3 chg-2 forward design notes (explicit Status: IN_REVIEW per anti-pattern; risk-case enumeration). Documents non-override of BOTH iter-3 chg-1 high_cost_check AND iter-5 chg-3 pediatric_complex check (GC-012 is the canonical interaction case). PROMPT_REGISTRY DecisionSupportAgent v2.4 -> v2.5.
changes.failure_pattern: Phase H2 expansion risk: a third memory entry could (a) over-fire on mild pediatric asthma cases by recognizing the indication shape and skipping severity verification, or (b) override the pediatric_complex check on GC-012-style cases. Both classes are guarded against by the entry's structure (explicit severity requirements, explicit non-override documentation) and verified live.
changes.root_cause: Phase H2 is iterative by design; each new entry must respect contracts established by prior entries + by ClinicalRiskDetector's pre-flight checks. Adding entries proves the methodology generalizes across disease/biologic families.

evidence.note: Third entry: 5 required criteria (severe persistent asthma + named GINA step, high-dose ICS/LABA with adequate trial, eosinophilic phenotype with eos+FeNO thresholds, age >= 12, no contraindication), 5 anti-patterns each with Status: IN_REVIEW (Not DENIED), explicit dual-policy-check non-override paragraph naming GC-012.
4 new test classes for the asthma entry: injection / criterion preservation / anti-patterns / pediatric_complex interaction. Aggregate H2 tests: 51.

GC-012 IN_REVIEW via pediatric_complex (memory did NOT override the policy check) score 4. GC-023 AUTO_APPROVED (memory did NOT over-fire on mild) score 5; judge: 'correctly avoiding the anti-pattern of escalating based solely on age'.

changes.why_this_component: Third H2 entry; same constraint level as iter-3 chg-2 (NSCLC) and iter-4 chg-1 (RA). Asthma chosen as the next-most-defensible target because GC-012 provides a canonical case where multiple pre-flight checks interact with the memory, giving the cleanest test yet of the 'memory as support, not replacement' contract.
changes.rollback_plan: git revert <sha>. Removing the third entry from long_term_memory.md shrinks the rendered memory section; PROMPT_REGISTRY version reverts to v2.4.

verdicts.notes: iter-4 chg-1 (RA biologic H2 memory entry) had four named risk cases. All four preserved at iter-5 HEAD with the third H2 entry now active alongside: GC-010 IN_REVIEW via cost, GC-005 IN_REVIEW psoriasis, GC-017 IN_REVIEW PsA, GC-016 AUTO_APPROVED Crohn's. The criterion-preservation tests for all 3 entries pass — adding the asthma entry did not displace the RA entry's criteria or anti-patterns. Confirmed keep.
iter-4 chg-2 (decision_agent.py deletion) had no risk cases and no predictions. Full suite remains green at iter-5 HEAD; the file remains deleted with no importers. Confirmed keep.
